issues.description: WebSocket endpoint /ws/build/{sid} accepts auth token from Authorization header with no rate limiting — an unauthenticated attacker can enumerate session IDs and read build output from any session. The WS endpoint is completely outside the require_auth dependency chain.
Telegram webhook /webhook/telegram has no rate limiting (no Depends(require_rate_limit)) — the endpoint is publicly reachable and can be flooded to trigger mark_alert_acked calls in a tight loop.

Gap audit confirms Linear two-way sync (build outcomes posted back to Linear) is NOT implemented — sessions complete but Linear issues are never transitioned to Done/In Review automatically. This breaks the core autonomy loop claim.

claude-agent-sdk>=0.1 is an unpinned version with no lock file — any breaking change in a minor release will silently break all SDK paths. CLAUDE.md states TAO_USE_AGENT_SDK=0 causes ImportError, meaning a bad SDK publish makes the entire server unbootable.

_telegram_send() uses urllib.request with a 8s timeout but is called synchronously inside an async route handler — this blocks the event loop for up to 8 seconds on every /ack_alert call if Telegram is slow or unreachable.

scan endpoint `asyncio.create_task(_run())` discards the task reference — if the scan raises an exception after returning 200, the error is silently swallowed with no way to surface it to the caller or Supabase.

_IS_CLOUD detection (RAILWAY_ENVIRONMENT / RENDER / FLY_APP_NAME env vars) controls cookie SameSite/Secure. On Railway this is correct, but any other HTTPS deployment that doesn't set these vars will issue SameSite=Strict cookies that break cross-origin auth from the Vercel dashboard.

NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL appears twice with different values — once pointing to the real project URL (zbryrmxmgfmslqzizsto.supabase.co) and once as a placeholder (your-project.supabase.co). This will cause copy-paste errors where the real service role key is paired with a wrong URL.

POST /api/webhook handles both GitHub and Linear webhooks in a single 130+ line function with deeply nested if/elif branches. Missing webhook secret on GitHub path raises HTTP 500 (config misconfiguration leaks internals) rather than a documented error.

Two Dockerfiles exist (root Dockerfile and app/Dockerfile) with divergent WORKDIR, ENV, and COPY instructions. Root Dockerfile copies src/ and .harness/ which app/Dockerfile does not — production deployments from the wrong Dockerfile will silently miss TAO engine files.

SDK version policy references ClaudeSDKClient.receive_messages() and ClaudeSDKClient.query() as stable API surface, but CLAUDE.md and board meeting notes reference the SDK as claude_agent_sdk and claude-agent-sdk interchangeably — the actual installed package name is ambiguous and the import contract is unverified against the public SDK.

run_spec, run_plan, run_test endpoints use background_tasks.add_task(_run) where _run is a synchronous wrapper calling blocking I/O (run_spec_phase, run_plan_phase etc) — FastAPI BackgroundTasks runs in the same event loop thread pool, so long-running sync tasks will starve async request handling.

GET /api/monitor/digest opens a file inside the request handler synchronously (open(files[0])) — blocks the async event loop on disk I/O, particularly problematic if the digest file is large (monitor digests can grow).

dev script uses bash -c 'source .env.local' which silently does nothing on Windows (PowerShell/cmd) — the README Quick Start shows PowerShell commands, creating a platform mismatch that will break local dev on Windows.
ruff version is pinned (ruff==0.15.10) but pytest is unpinned (pytest>=8) — any pytest major release can break the test suite in CI without any human decision to upgrade.

Smoke test workflow starts the server with python -m uvicorn server.main:app but does not set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — on startup main.py logs a WARNING about missing key, and any test that exercises the SDK path will fail silently rather than loudly.

fallback-dryrun-quarterly trigger uses day_of_month + month fields but the cron.py dispatcher (referenced in config.yaml) only shows hour/minute/weekday handling in the board meeting minutes. The quarterly trigger pattern may not be implemented in the actual cron loop.
on_event('startup') and on_event('shutdown') decorators are deprecated in FastAPI 0.111+ in favour of lifespan context managers — will generate deprecation warnings in logs on every startup.

CLAUDE.md states subprocess fallback removed in RA-576 and TAO_USE_AGENT_SDK=0 causes ImportError, but app/requirements.txt still lists claude-agent-sdk>=0.1 without any guard — if the package is not installed, the ImportError fires at import time with no useful diagnostic message pointing to requirements.txt.

Anthropic docs stored as raw HTML/JS bundle from server-side rendering — the files are thousands of lines of Next.js RSC JSON and CSS, not readable documentation. The intel refresh mechanism is storing the wrong content format.

missingTests: WebSocket /ws/build/{sid} authentication bypass — no test verifies that an invalid token is rejected at the WS upgrade
Rate limiting enforcement — no test verifies that the 30 req/min limit actually blocks a 31st request
Telegram webhook secret validation — no test for the case where TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET is set but header is absent
Linear webhook HMAC verification path — no integration test for the /api/webhook Linear branch
Session resume endpoint — no test for POST /api/sessions/{sid}/resume with various status values
Parallel build fan-out /api/build/parallel — no test for n_workers validation or actual fan-out behaviour
Cron trigger persistence — no test verifying that cron-triggers.json survives a server restart and triggers re-register correctly
SDK fallback path — no test for what happens when claude_agent_sdk import fails at runtime
Supabase write failure isolation — no test confirming that a Supabase error does NOT propagate to block a build session
Docker build correctness — no CI job builds either Dockerfile to verify the image actually starts
securityConcerns: app/server/main.py: Telegram bot token (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN) is logged via log.warning if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is missing — if log aggregation is misconfigured, token may appear in plaintext logs

app/server/main.py: /webhook/telegram processes /ack_alert commands that call mark_alert_acked(alert_key) — alert_key comes directly from Telegram message text with no sanitisation or length limit before being passed to the Supabase writer
app/server/main.py: Security CSP header allows script-src 'unsafe-inline' — weakens XSS protection for the static frontend served from /static

app/server/main.py: ALLOWED_ORIGINS list is built from TAO_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var by splitting on comma with no URL validation — a misconfigured value like 'https://evil.com' is silently added to the CORS allowlist

.claude/settings.json: defaultMode bypassPermissions is committed to the repository — any developer who runs Claude CLI in this working directory gets unrestricted tool access including Write(*) and rm:*
app/requirements.txt: claude-agent-sdk>=0.1 is completely unpinned — a malicious or buggy publish to PyPI could compromise all build sessions since the SDK runs arbitrary code in the workspace
.env.example: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is documented in the root .env.example — the service role key bypasses RLS and its presence in a template file increases the risk of accidental commit

app/server/main.py: _telegram_send helper uses urllib.request without certificate verification options — on Railway the default Python cert bundle applies, but if REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE is misconfigured, MITM is possible
positives: app/server/main.py: Pydantic field_validator on all request models provides robust input validation with clear error messages — repo_url prefix check and model allowlist are exactly right
app/server/main.py: _resilient() wrapper with crash-recovery and auto-restart for all background tasks is a genuinely good pattern — prevents silent background task death
app/server/main.py: SecurityHeaders middleware applies CSP, X-Frame-Options, and X-XSS-Protection consistently on every response — not bolted on per-route

app/server/main.py: /health endpoint exposes autonomy.armed, seconds_since_last_poll, and disk_free_gb — this is unusually honest health reporting that distinguishes process liveness from operational readiness
CLAUDE.md: Explicit persistence guidelines (write-to-.tmp-then-os.replace) for atomic file writes shows real production operational thinking
.github/workflows/ci.yml: Three-job CI (python + frontend + smoke-prod) with a post-deploy gate against Railway on main-branch pushes is a mature CI setup for a solo project
app/server/main.py: Webhook deduplication via _find_active_session_for_repo prevents double-triggering from rapid GitHub pushes — prevents a common race condition in webhook-driven CI systems
.harness/ESCALATION.md: Explicit list of 9 things the watchdog will NEVER do autonomously is an excellent operational guardrail — demonstrates genuine understanding of autonomous system failure modes
dashboard/tsconfig.json: strict mode enabled with no exceptions — combined with the no-any convention in CLAUDE.md this is a genuinely tight TypeScript configuration
